tr.td: Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Erythrocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood by Automated count
MCV [Entitic mean volume] in Red Blood Cells by Automated count
MCHC [Entitic Mass/volume] in Red Blood Cells by Automated count
Erythrocyte distribution width [Entitic volume] by Automated count
Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Platelet distribution width [Entitic volume] in Blood by Automated count
Platelet [Entitic mean volume] in Blood by Automated count
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Laboratory results are largely within normal limits. Mildly elevated chloride and low CO₂ may suggest a mild metabolic acidosis. Erythrocyte count is slightly low, but other red cell indices are normal, indicating no significant anemia. Platelet distribution width is elevated, which may reflect increased platelet size variability but platelet count is normal. No evidence of renal dysfunction, dyslipidemia, or impaired glucose control. Clinical correlation recommended.

text.div: Provider Organization: Punkt Lekarski w Cieksynie
